summary:
You are given a list of meeting time intervals, each written as [start, end]. Determine whether a single person could attend every meeting, meaning no two meetings overlap in time.

Return true if all meetings can be attended, or false if any pair of them clashes.

examples.explanation: The meeting [0, 30] overlaps both [5, 10] and [15, 20], so they cannot all be attended.
[2, 4] ends before [7, 10] begins, so there is no conflict.

constraints: Each interval is [start, end] with start <= end
An empty list of meetings returns true
Meetings that only touch at an endpoint (one ends exactly when the next starts) are not in conflict
This is a LeetCode Premium problem
